commands.review_summary:
The PR decodes Antigravity quota-summary `resetTime` into `RateWindow.resetsAt`, renders those extra-window resets through the shared formatter, and extends quota-summary parser tests.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible, not live-reproduced: current main does not decode `resetTime` and sets quota-summary `RateWindow.resetsAt` to `nil`, so a payload with `resetTime` cannot render structured reset text through that path.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the next step is ordinary maintainer review of the small provider parsing/UI change and posted proof.

target.title: feat: parse and format resetTime for local Antigravity quota summary
target.body: ### Summary Completes the Antigravity quota summary restructuring from PR #1509. Prior to this change, local status probes (`RetrieveUserQuotaSummary`) did not parse the `resetTime` field, leaving `RateWindow.resetsAt` as `nil` and relying on brittle parsing of the English `resetDescription` text. This change: - Decodes the `resetTime` ISO8601 string from the local quota summary JSON payload. - Populates `RateWindow.resetsAt` with the parsed date. - Renders the structured reset time via the unified `resetText` formatter in `MenuCardView+ModelHelpers`, ensuring proper localization and formatting. ### Commands Run - `make check` - `swift test --filter Antigravity` ### UI / Screenshots | Before | After | | --- | --- | | | | - Local LSP/CLI status now displays structured reset times (e.g., "Resets tomorrow, 12:39 AM" or "Resets Jun 19 at 4:45 PM") rather than falling back to raw English description text. - OAuth remains unchanged (retains its model-level structured reset times).
target.author: akunzai
